methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Cemvita is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Moji and Tara Karimi that has raised $30M to date. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), including later-stage climate-tech firms such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $30M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $57M. Peer-median benchmarking draws from three same-sector, adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.0B and a range of $700M to $3.0B, implying $1.0B for Cemvita. The peer set spans Series C financings, placing them one stage ahead of the target.

Triangulating these methods yields a valuation range of $48M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $528M. The wide dispersion reflects material stage-gap uncertainty between Series B and the Series C peer set. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative floor anchored to capital deployed, while peer-median signals sector appetite and later-stage exit multiples but introduces overfitting risk given the limited peer sample and single stage remove. Key uncertainties include technology inflection risk in climate tech, peer comparability on product maturity, and market conditions for Series C deployment.
